summary: Error 126 or Crash on startup when a GDExtention depends on another GDExtention (Windows)

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproductible in :
- v4.5.stable.official [876b29033]

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6590) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-4770K CPU @ 3.50GHz (8 threads) - 31.94 GiB memory  - Python 3.12.3 - SCons: v4.7.0.265be6883fadbb5a545612265acc919595158366

### Issue description

I'm trying to make a GDExtension (called "main") that depends on another GDExtension (called "dependency").
Both extensions build successfully with SCons, but when launching the editor, Godot reports that the main DLL cannot be found:
```
ERROR: Can't open dynamic library: D:/GodotProjects/mrp-gdextention-dependency/main_extension/bin/libmain.windows.template_debug.x86_64.dll. Error: Error 126: The specified module could not be found..
   at: (platform/windows/os_windows.cpp:533)
ERROR: Can't open GDExtension dynamic library: 'res://main_extension/bin/main.gdextension'.
   at: (core/extension/gdextension.cpp:728)
```
Full log here : [err126.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22922480/err126.log)

From what I found online, Error 126 usually means that a dependency of the DLL could not be found — in this case, likely `libdependency.windows.template_debug.x86_64.dll` (the DLL from the "dependency" GDExtension).
However, my `main.gdextension` file correctly declares this dependency:
```
[configuration]
entry_symbol = "main_library_init"
compatibility_minimum = "4.1"
reloadable = true

[libraries]
windows.debug.x86_64 = "res://main_extension/bin/libmain.windows.template_debug.x86_64.dll"

[dependencies]
windows.debug.x86_64 = {
	"res://dependency_extension/bin/libdependency.windows.template_debug.x86_64.dll": ""
}
```

I tried several strategies to ensure `libdependency.windows.template_debug.x86_64.dll` could be found:
- Adding the dependency folder to my system `PATH`.
- Calling `SetDllDirectory()` inside `main_library_init()`.

Neither worked.

As a last attempt, I copied `libdependency.windows.template_debug.x86_64.dll` into the same folder as `libmain.windows.template_debug.x86_64.dll`.
This time, Godot crashes at startup.
Full crash log here: [crash.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22922493/crash.log)

### Steps to reproduce

Precompiled libraries are also provided in the MRP for those who want to reproduce the issue without setting up SCons.

## Without scons :
1) Open the project in Godot
=> The console shows :
```
ERROR: Can't open dynamic library: D:/GodotProjects/mrp-gdextention-dependency/main_extension/bin/libmain.windows.template_debug.x86_64.dll. Error: Error 126: The specified module could not be found..
   at: (platform/windows/os_windows.cpp:533)
ERROR: Can't open GDExtension dynamic library: 'res://main_extension/bin/main.gdextension'.
   at: (core/extension/gdextension.cpp:728)
```

2) Copy `res://dependency_extension/bin/libdependency.windows.template_debug.x86_64.dll` into `res://main_extension/bin`
3) Open the project in Godot
=> The editor crashes at startup

## With SCons :
1) Delete `res://dependency_extension/bin/libdependency.windows.template_debug.x86_64.dll` and `res://main_extension/bin/libmain.windows.template_debug.x86_64.dll`
2) Rebuild the dependency gdextension :
	From a terminal at the root of the project :
	```
	$ cd dependency_extension/
	$ scons platform=windows
	```
3) Rebuild the main gdextension :
	From a terminal at the root of the project :
	```
	$ cd min_extension/
	$ scons platform=windows
	```
4) Open the project in Godot:
=> The console shows :
```
ERROR: Can't open dynamic library: D:/GodotProjects/mrp-gdextention-dependency/main_extension/bin/libmain.windows.template_debug.x86_64.dll. Error: Error 126: The specified module could not be found..
   at: (platform/windows/os_windows.cpp:533)
ERROR: Can't open GDExtension dynamic library: 'res://main_extension/bin/main.gdextension'.
   at: (core/extension/gdextension.cpp:728)
```
5) Copy `res://dependency_extension/bin/libdependency.windows.template_debug.x86_64.dll` into `res://main_extension/bin`
6) Open the project in Godot:
=> The editor crashes at startup

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

godot-cpp-4.5 is required to build the GDExtensions.
Because the repository is large (~282 MB), I didn’t include it as an archive.
Instead, you can clone the MRP from my public repository directly:
```
git clone https://github.com/J-Ponzo/mrp-gdextention-dependency.git
```

Or download the archive from this link : https://github.com/J-Ponzo/mrp-gdextention-dependency/archive/refs/heads/main.zip

summary: 🐛 Bug Report: Saving Twice Quickly (Ctrl+S) Can Corrupt Scene File in Godot 4.4.1

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: 4.4.1.stable.nixpkgs.49a5bc7b6

Not tested in: earlier 4.3.x or 4.2.x builds

Not tested above: versions newer than 4.4.1 (no later builds installed)

### System information

OS: Linux (NixOS, Nixpkgs build) Godot Engine v4.4.1.stable.nixpkgs.49a5bc7b6 – https://godotengine.org  CPU: Intel Core i5-13600KF (13th Gen, 20 threads @ 5.10 GHz) GPU: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 (Lite Hash Rate) Driver: NVIDIA 570.181 (CUDA 12.8) Renderer: Vulkan 1.4.303 – Forward+ Memory: 64 GB DDR4 RAM Storage: NVMe SSD (/dev/nvme0n1, 54 TB read / 28 TB written, 8 % wear, 0 media errors)

### Issue description

If you press Ctrl+S twice in quick succession while saving a scene or project, the scene file can become completely corrupted.
It looks like the engine starts a second save before the first one finishes, and the file gets zeroed out or truncated, resulting in total data loss.

No warning or error message appears, and there’s no automatic backup or recovery. This happens consistently when saving large scenes or on slower disks where the first save takes more than a moment.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open any project in Godot 4.4.1.

2. Open a scene and make a small change.
 
3. Press Ctrl+S to save.
 
4. Immediately press Ctrl+S again before the save completes.
 
5. Wait for it to finish, then close and reopen the scene file.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

basically any project

summary: Enabling SMAA breaks FSR2 upscaling, resulting in blurry visuals

description.text: - *Related to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/68096.* ### Tested versions - Reproducible in: 4.5.dev4 (first version with SMAA), 4.5.stable, 4.6.dev e82516995 ### System information Godot v4.6.dev (e82516995) - Fedora Linux 42 (KDE Plasma Desktop Edition) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 (nvidia; 580.95.05) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 62.33 GiB memory ### Issue description Enabling SMAA breaks FSR2 upscaling, presumably due to running the SMAA pass at the internal resolution, but *after* upscaling. Instead, SMAA should run *before* upscaling, but still at the internal resolution (so that it can effectively catch aliasing that would be gone after upscaling). In contrast, FXAA handles this correctly, probably because it's a single-pass filter as opposed to SMAA's multi-pass approach. Screenshots with **Scaling 3D > Scale** set to `0.25`: No spatial AA | FXAA | SMAA (blurry) -|-|- | | The SMAA screenshot should look roughly as sharp as the FXAA one (probably sharper, given SMAA doesn't blur the image as much). ### Steps to reproduce - Enable SMAA and set **Scaling 3D > Mode** to FSR2 in the Project Settings. - Set **Scaling 3D > Scale** to a low value like `0.25`. - Notice how rendering becomes very blurry (even in still scenes) until you disable SMAA. Normally, FSR2 allows the scene to look reasonably sharp at low resolution scale factors, at least in still scenes. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [test_fsr2_smaa.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22909153/test_fsr2_smaa.zip)

summary: Fix opening found lines in built-in scripts

description.text:
Fixes #75600

The fix was actually very easy. When a line is found in a built-in script, it will be a specific line in the scene file. That line will always be preceded by a script header from the script it's part of. So I just made the editor crawl upwards until the header, which allows to determine the correct script. Previously the scanning was done from the top and it only found the first script or whatever.

For some reason, clicking the line if script editor is not visible, will not jump to that line. You have to click again. Not sure how to fix that, as using deferred call does not help. It's not that important though, code search is usually done from within script editor anyway.

summary: Calling `notify_property_list_changed` collapses object properties in Array of custom Ressources

### Issue description

I'm trying to write a tool script that has an array of custom ressources whose contents will dynamically update enum options for other objects. 
To do that, my understanding is that the best approach is to have each object signal it has changed when its properties are modified, and then to recalculate the enum options using `notify_property_list_changed()`. 
However, while that approach works, every time `notify_property_list_changed()` is called, object's properties in the array get collapsed in the editor, meaning I can only change the properties 1 character at a time.

- Expand the Array Of Custom
- Add an element

summary: User defined default project directory for Project Manager

description.text:
This PR aims to allow the user to set the default location of where new projects are created. 

[Screencast_20251014_011447.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/677853df-7b85-4cb2-b598-a5159eb6c8cb)

I've marked it as draft as it needs testing, and I'm unsure if this is the right way of implementing it.

All references to `filesystem/directories/default_project_path` have been changed to `../user_defined_project_path`.
default_project_path is now _always_ the Godot default location, this is so the user can delete their custom directory and see a placeholder of where the original directory was.

`user_defined_project_path` is set to the same value as default_project_path on creation, but can be changed via the settings menu. 
In theory, this shouldn't cause any loss of functionality for users used to this, and should only serve to improve the experience.

I still need to add translations (or well, the support for such) and change the naming(?), perhaps calling the user_defined option the "default" in the settings menu is misleading? Even though it is true...
Or perhaps call user_defined -> default, and call default... something else I guess?

I'm also toying with the idea of adding a reset button that resets the path to the true default, but given no other option has this functionality I'm unsure if it'd fit.

summary: Support Thumb Buttons for FileSystem dock

description.text:
I'm a huge fan of the bottom docked FileSystem with the big icon layout, but something I noticed was that thumb button navigation wasn't implemented. 
This PR resolves that by allowing the user to navigate through the FileSystem via thumb buttons, much like some file explorers and web browsers. 
The Forward button (Mouse5) moves you forward in history, this is identical to pressing the forward button at the top left.
The Back button (Mouse4) moves you backwards, this too, is identical to the behaviour caused by the backwards button. 

[Screencast_20251013_230324.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9fef3581-939e-44fb-9cec-80e966c8b01f)

A neat unintended side-effect is that since you no longer need to navigate by clicking, this lets you drag items through folders while pressing these buttons.

_
Do note that this doesn't include support for file dialogs, I'm unsure if that is within the scope of the PR as the file dialog does things a bit differently, such as having a shortcut for back/forward within alt+left and alt+right.
It would need to be up for discussion on whether or not these shortcuts should also apply to the FileSystem dock.
In my opinion, ideally M5/M4 are default shortcut keys alongside alt+left/alt+right, which would give the user the ability to disable it if they wish. The issue is that the shortcut system doesn't seem setup to support mouse inputs, which is probably for the better given the mouse literally only has 2 real bindable buttons. 

Edit:
It seems #65790 would actually allow for the shortcut method to be an option, seems to be stuck though.

[^1]: The contributions of this PR (adding a single button to the sidebar) are technically different from the linked proposal (making the folder icon on each project item row stand out more). However, the PR accomplishes the same underlying goal in my opinion (making it easier for users to open a project folder).

summary: AnimationTree State Machine node UX improvements

description.text:
This PR includes various UX improvements to how nodes in the AnimationTree's State Machine GUI functions. 

### Control Dragging
Node dragging via control/command is now *properly* supported. This feature allows you to click anywhere on a node and begin dragging it, this is primarily useful due to state machine nodes being 90% text.
Prior to this PR, this functionality existed, but the UX on it was lacking. 

(note: in both videos I am holding the control key)
Before:

[Screencast_20251013_113955.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e768cfe1-b3bf-420a-b40a-0dfd85382a45)

After:

[Screencast_20251013_114038.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/16f5a0aa-9911-4e09-a5fe-aa768b2b9a4d)

When holding the control key,  the cursor IBeam shape and button highlights no longer show, as they aren't functional when control dragging. 
You can now re-pick up a node by simply dragging it again, before you had to go through a mandatory deselect
Fixes the selection box appearing despite dragging a node.

### Selection Box

Selection box behaviour prior to this PR was weird and inconsistent with how the main blend tree behaved, this PR fixes those issues, and it's behaviour is now consistent with other Node Graph GUIs.

Before:

[Screencast_20251013_114233.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6ec74fec-249f-4f16-b85d-1f6b22770c5d)

After:

[Screencast_20251013_114302.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4e2e958b-475e-4ed6-93e0-46fe9c6b0d12)

Shift selection now only *removes* nodes from the selection.
Control/Command selection now only *adds* nodes to the selection.

**Note**: This does *remove* the ability to invert selection, I don't know if anyone was using this functionality, but given this doesn't exist in the blend tree I found this to be unexpected behaviour. 

Additionally, the small flicker that occurs when re-selection happens has been fixed, it's now as smooth as ever.

summary: CodeEdit: Use flag to recalculate characteristics

description.text:
Completion options would store old matching information, just to check it against the current match information. If they don't match sorting characteristics need to be recalculated (since options which don't fit in at the current cursor position rank lower).

This PR replaces the stored old matches with a dirty flag, that is set to true when matches are changed. This removes some overhead from moving around and comparing the vectors.

Without this PR, characteristics would not be recalculated, when matches were updated but stayed identical. With this PR they always will be.
However, in reality it's highly unlikely for the match list to stay identical, since an update only happens for the following:
- a "user" update (`request_code_completion`) -> all options get replaced by entirely new computed options -> matches change in any case
- a cursor movement -> the cursor position influences the matches, since only characters before the cursor have to match
- adding or deleting characters -> changes the string (source code) which is matched against

So just flagging on any change seems fine to me.

### Benchmark

Actually benchmarking this is quite hard, since finding a realistic workload isn't easy. My attempt at a benchmark uses 3000 options, which have prefixes, that control whether the option matches or not (20% matches, 80% does not). And just appends and uses random numbers for some arguments.

<details>
<summary>Benchmark script</summary>

```gdscript
var edit := CodeEdit.new()
var random = RandomNumberGenerator.new()

func _ready() -> void:
	add_child(edit)
	
	edit.text = "o"
	random.seed = hash("elpsykongroo")
	edit.add_caret(0, 1)
	
	benchmark(100)

func trigger():
	for i in range(3000):
		if random.randf() < 0.2:
			# create a match
			edit.add_code_completion_option(CodeEdit.KIND_PLAIN_TEXT, "option_" + str(i) + str(random.randi()), "option_" + str(i), Color.ALICE_BLUE, null, null, random.randi())
		else:
			# create a nonmatch
			edit.add_code_completion_option(CodeEdit.KIND_PLAIN_TEXT, "abc_" + str(i) + str(random.randi()), "abc_" + str(i), Color.ALICE_BLUE, null, null, random.randi())
	edit.update_code_completion_options(true)


func benchmark(n: int):
	var t1 = Time.get_ticks_usec()
	for i in range(n):
		trigger()
	var t2 = Time.get_ticks_usec()
	var delta = t2 - t1
	
	print("RL: %s usec (%s msec)" % [delta, delta / 1000])
```

</details>

Using this benchmark I see around a 4% speedup for "user" updates (`request_code_completion`).

If anyone has ideas for a better benchmark I'd love to hear it!

---

#### Why am I using "user" updates as the metric

My longterm goal would be, to let the script language recalculate completion options on every keystroke. Or speaking differently: I want to replace all internal CodeEdit sorting updates, with full user updates that pull in the current options from the script language.

So optimizing updates without a full replacement of the options isn't really worth it to me.

summary: Mouse coordinate desync in Embedded Game Window when viewport size differs from window size with Stretch Mode

description.text:
### Tested versions

**Versions where bug occurs:**
- Godot 4.4.stable ✅ (first version with Embedded Game Window feature)
- Godot 4.4.1.stable ✅
- Godot 4.5.stable ✅
- Godot 4.6.dev1 ✅

**Versions where feature does NOT exist:**
- Godot 4.3.stable and earlier ❌ (Embedded Game Window not available)

**Conclusion:** This bug has been present since the introduction of the Embedded Game Window feature in Godot 4.4 and affects all subsequent versions.

### System information

Windows 10 - Godot v4.5.stable - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1630 - Intel Core i5-10400F CPU @ 2.90GHz (12 Threads)

### Issue description

When using the Embedded Game Window with Stretch Mode enabled and ANY size mismatch between configured viewport size and actual window size, the mouse cursor teleports to incorrect positions after releasing right-click during camera movement with "Override in-game camera".

**Root cause:** The mouse coordinate transformation fails to properly handle scaling differences between the configured viewport size and the actual window size, regardless of whether the size difference comes from resolution override or manual window resizing.

**Expected behavior:** Mouse cursor should remain at the exact position where right-click was released, regardless of window/viewport size differences.

**Actual behavior:** Mouse cursor teleports to incorrect screen position proportional to the size scaling ratio.

### Steps to reproduce

**Prerequisites:**
- Godot 4.4+ (Embedded Game Window feature available)
- Any project (2D or 3D)

**Configuration Steps:**
1. Create new project
2. **Project Settings → Display → Window:**
   - Viewport Width: `1920`, Height: `1080`
   - Stretch Mode: `canvas_items` or `viewport`

3. **Editor setup:**
   - Enable **Embedded Game Window**
   - In embedded window toolbar: Activate **"3D"** and **"Override in-game camera"**

**Reproduction Steps:**
- **Method A (override):** Set ANY override values different from viewport size
- **Method B (resize):** Manually resize embedded window to ANY size different from viewport size
- Hold **right-click**
- Release **right-click**
- **Observe:** Mouse cursor teleports to incorrect position

**Root cause identified:**
The bug occurs whenever there's ANY mismatch between:
- **Configured viewport size** (e.g., 1920×1080)  
- **Actual window size** (via override OR manual resize)

**Important notes:**
- Bug occurs with ANY scene type (2D or 3D)
- Only requires "3D" view + "Override in-game camera" enabled
- Mouse coordinate system fails to account for size scaling differences

[EmbedWindowMouseBugProject.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22887284/EmbedWindowMouseBugProject.zip)

summary: X11: Fix fullscreen exit behavior

description.text:
Follow-up to #110990
Fixes #104071

Current problems:

- If a fullscreen window is minimized via code, unminimizing the window by clicking the window button on task bar brings the window to windowed mode instead of fullscreen.
- Making a maximized window fullscreen via code might show contents behind the window for a brief moment. This is more obvious if your window manager show animations for this kind of transitions.
- Each time you switch a fullscreen window back to windowed, the window is shifted by a fixed amount relative to the original position.

The first two problems have the same cause. It's because that we always exit fullscreen mode first when updating a window's mode, regardless of the target window mode. This PR changes it the exit only when necessary.

The last problem is more or less messed up, like the min/max issue last time.

In Godot, a window's position is the top-left corner of the client area (i.e., not including frame decorations like the title bar). In X11, the frame is not part of the window, the window manager adds the frame by reparenting the window to a dedicated frame window. 

When the frame is added, the window (client area) is shifted towards bottom-right by default. So subtracting by frame extents when setting the window's position lands at the desired position eventually :exploding_head: 

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/4219ce91f2d1fae65f289d27284a1ab35e9b7b5f/platform/linuxbsd/x11/display_server_x11.cpp#L2553-L2587

This makes sense in 3.x as the use case is very simple (getter + setter). However the window's position is cached in `WindowData` since 4.0. As one of the hardest problems in computer science, cache invalidation strikes, resulting in issues like https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/75292.

This PR reverts #76040 as it did not fix the core issue. The window still shifts when exiting fullscreen. (The direct cause is that X11 being client-server. We can only request exiting fullscreen, whether and when the exit happens is window manager dependent. In this case, restoring the saved window position gets a zero frame extent in `window_set_position()` since fullscreen is not yet removed at that point in time.)

Instead of fighting the system to make the cache up-to-date, it turns out that we can simply set the window's gravity in `WM_NORMAL_HINTS` to `Static` so that the window keeps its position when the frame is added. The code is a lot simpler :stuck_out_tongue:

This PR also removed `last_position_before_fs`. We don't need to save the window's original position before entering fullscreen and restore it after exiting. A EWMH-compliant window manager tracks and restores the geometry info for fullscreen windows anyway.

summary: Mouse button events arrive in random order on Mac with "Tap to click" enabled

description.text:
### Tested versions

happens in 4.4.1, 4.5, & 4.5.1-rc2

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - macOS Sequoia (15.3.2) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M4 (Apple9) - Apple M4 (10 threads) - 16.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

On Mac, with System Settings > Trackpad > Tap to click enabled, tapping the trackpad makes 2 `InputEventMouseButton` events, `pressed=true` and `pressed=false`, in quick succession. If you tap an `Area3D`, sometimes `_unhandled_input` will be called twice (`true` then `false`), and then the clicked `Area3D` will be called twice (`true` then `false`); other times, seemingly randomly, `_unhandled_input` will be called (`true`), then `Area3D` (`true`), then `_unhandled_input` (`false`), then `Area3D` (`false`).

summary: NodePath Slice Method Missing in C# API

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Tested on Godot 4.4
- Based on my reading of the Godot source, this issue exists currently in Godot 4.5 and also in previous versions: Godot 4.3 and at least some prior versions.

### System information

Windows 11 - Godot 4.4 - Forward+ renderer - NVIDIA GPU GeForce RTX 3050 Laptop

### Issue description

According to [NodePath's Documentation](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/classes/class_nodepath.html#class-nodepath-method-slice), NodePath should have a method called `slice`. Indeed, it does have that method if you are developing with GDScript. However, if a developer wishes to use the C# version of `Slice`, the developer will find that their code will not compile. Apparently, the `Slice` method does not exist in the C# `NodePath` API.

Given the documentation, the C# version of `NodePath` should have a `Slice` method.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new project in Godot Mono.
2. Go to **Tools > C# > Create C# Solution**
3. Create a new C# Script.
4. Add a method that takes a `NodePath` as a parameter
5. Attempt to call `Slice` on that parameter.
6. Attempt to build the project.

The build will fail.

You can also create a similar GDScript that also calls `NodePath`'s `slice` method to verify that it is present in the GDScript version.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[TestCSharp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22868482/TestCSharp.zip)

This project includes both a C# and a GDScript variant to demonstrate the differences in the APIs.

summary: Crash with no debug info from overridden type mismatch

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.5.0 and 4.4.1.

### System information

Tested on both Windows 10 and Zorin Linux distro

### Issue description

The engine crashes with no debug information when a child class's override method signature does not match the parent method's signature, and the returned value is null.

From my perspective, there are two obvious possible fixes. I'd be fine with either.
1. This should be a compiler error, or at least a warning. The child class's method should be marked as needing to have the same return type as (or ideally, any subtype of) the parent class's.
2. When a crash like this happens, an exception should be thrown that indicates the chain of calls which failed and why, instead of a silent crash.

Thank you for your time.

### Steps to reproduce

ParentClass.gd:
```
class_name ParentClass

func get_array() -> Array:
	return []
```

ChildClass.gd:
```
extends ParentClass

class_name ChildClass

func get_array():
	return null
```

Code that crashes:
```
var my_array_getter: ParentClass = ChildClass.new()

func on_button_press():
	var thing := my_array_getter.get_array()
	if thing == null:
		print("Thing is null")
		return
	
	print(str(thing.size()) + " // " + str(thing))
```

From my debugging, please note the following happens:
1. `thing` gets set to null, despite being of type 'Array`.
2. The if-check fails, and decides that `thing != null`, even though in the debugger, the value of `thing` is `<null>`.
3. The crash happens on the `print` line.

I had previously thought I was safe because the `if` should have caught this, but in practice it fails.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[empty-array-crash-test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22867633/empty-array-crash-test.zip)

summary: Fix TextEdit selecting when closing popup

description.text:
- Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/95471 
- Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/104554 (probably)
- Fixes clicking and dragging from the line fold gutter or breakpoint gutter extends a selection from the previous caret position. (The line number gutter still starts line selection mode).
- Fixes triple-clicking line selection mode with multiple selections.
- Fixes selecting continues when alt tabbing and mouse is released.

Clicking on the TextEdit while there is a popup open now does nothing in the TextEdit (when embed subwindows is off), which is normal behavior for Controls.

I made the `_update_selection_mode_pointer` now use the word begin/end column, since it is now possible to start a word selection (double click) and then change it to a regular selection with shift+click. Otherwise, if the selection is extended above the word, the word would not be selected anymore. I used the selection origin determining code from `_update_selection_mode_word` for this.

summary: Depict version discrepancies in Project Manager

description.text:
Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/13350 .

This PR adds an icon next to each project's version number in the Project Manager if the project's version is different from the Project Manager version.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/82f5b3bd-ac63-4a1e-a5fa-8150d19952bb

If a project uses an *older* version of Godot than the PM's version, it will show an up arrow, indicating that opening the project will upgrade it. Conversely, if the project's version is *newer* than the PM's version, a down arrow will indicate it will be downgraded. If the project version matches the PM version, no icon is displayed.

Other unsupported project features remain attached to the existing ⚠️ icon.

---

I chose the current up and down icons since they already existed in Godot and can be interpreted as "upgrade" and "downgrade" fairly easily. However, I do wish they stood out more (especially "downgrade" being a dangerous action, as it is right now with the ⚠️ symbol); perhaps we can find or come up with a better icon to indicate this.

summary: Billboarding broken at far distances from origin

### Tested versions

summary: AnimationPlayer.play_section_with_markers bug while playing between 2 section

description.text: ### Tested versions Godot Engine v4.5.stable.steam.876b29033 - https://godotengine.org Vulkan 1.4.312 - Forward+ - Using Device #0: NVIDIA - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4090 ### System information Godot Engine v4.5.stable.steam.876b29033 - https://godotengine.org Vulkan 1.4.312 - Forward+ - Using Device #0: NVIDIA - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4090 ### Issue description When a section that is later in the timeline finishes playing, and then a section that is earlier in the timeline is played subsequently, the AnimationPlayer will only play the last frame of the earlier section directly, instead of playing the section normally from start to end. ### Steps to reproduce For example, I divided an animation into two sections using a casting marker: The section from frame 0 to the casting marker is named Section A. The section from the casting marker to the end of the animation is named Section B. Normal Behavior (Expected) When I play Section A first and then play Section B, the animation works correctly: it starts playing from frame 0, proceeds to the casting marker (completing Section A), and then continues playing from the casting marker to the end of the animation (completing Section B). Abnormal Behavior (Bug) However, if I attempt to play Section A again right after Section B finishes, an unexpected issue occurs: the AnimationPlayer seems to directly seek to the casting marker (the end boundary of Section A) instead of playing Section A normally from frame 0 to the casting marker. As a result, Section A fails to play in full and only the final frame of the section is displayed. ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0d4630bf-3df8-4a09-9347-f85ac09d17bf) ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) ```gdscript extends Node3D @export var animation_player: AnimationPlayer @export var label_cur_pressed: Label func _input(event: InputEvent) -> void: if event.is_action_pressed("ui_up"): animation_player.play_section_with_markers("mixamo_com", "", "casting") label_cur_pressed.text = "up" if event.is_action_pressed("ui_down"): animation_player.play_section_with_markers("mixamo_com", "casting", "") label_cur_pressed.text = "down" if event.is_action_pressed("ui_left"): animation_player.play_section_with_markers("mixamo_com", "", "2") ``` MRP still too big to upload. You can simply make your own test project.

### Tested versions

-It only happens since i updated to 4.5.

### System information

Windows 11

### Issue description

Since i updated to Godot 4.5 i get this error when i hover the cursor over some of the dropdown menu buttons in the inspector: ERROR: Formatting error in string "Bezárja és menti a változásokat?": not all arguments converted during string formatting.
It's not during testing or anything, just in the engine itself.
I can use english (and the error isn't present there), but it could be more annoying to others.
As i understand it's just some formatting error with the line "Bezárja és menti a változásokat?", which could be the is "Close and save?" in english.

### Steps to reproduce

-Use hungarian language.
-Select a node.
-Hover over the properties dropdown menu buttons in the inspector, in which something is not on the default value (e.g. the positon is modified).

summary: Revert "Handle NaN and Infinity in JSON stringify function"

description.text:
Reverts godotengine/godot#108837 which was merged in error. The core team discussed it on September 17 2025 and agreed that it should not be merged as is. Further any attempt at slightly improving the behaviour here will likely create just as many problems since we are working outside the boundaries of the JSON spec and there is no consensus on how NaN and INF should be treated by other programs. Any option is liable to break more JSON parsers than it appeases. 

Therefore we agreed to do the following:
1. Have a big and notable warning whenever a users tries to save an invalid JSON
2. Output something that can be parsed by a notable JSON parsing system (i.e. match how Python or C# handles these cases. 

We agreed not to try to handle this in a unique way that we think is slightly better than what everyone else has done and instead try to match the behaviour of another JSON parser.
